channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Migrants not entitled to apply for asylum while standing on Mexico side of border, Supreme Court rules
item.dc:creator: Julian Mark
item.description: The 6-3 decision clears the way for the Trump administration to resume allowing federal agents at the border to turn back asylum seekers before they enter.

item.title: Israel deliberately targeted Palestinian children in Gaza, U.N. commission says
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih
item.description: The Israeli Foreign Ministry called the findings a “libelous sham.”

item.title: Hegseth thwarted internal efforts to extend key Army general’s career
item.dc:creator: Dan Lamothe
item.description: Gen. Christopher Donahue, seen as a top warfighter, is the latest apparent casualty in a purge of senior military leaders by the Trump administration.

item.title: France reports first Ebola patient as cases in Africa surge above 1,000
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor
item.description: France said a doctor back from a humanitarian mission was infected and in stable condition, while experts said the outbreak in Africa could be the worst ever recorded.

item.title: Ukraine’s drones are spoiling Putin’s vision for Crimea
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, David Stern, Natalia Abbakumova
item.description: Russian-installed authorities in Crimea have halted fuel distribution in vacation season as Ukraine isolates the peninsula.

item.title: Trump dismisses Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspections
item.dc:creator: Adam Taylor, Victoria Craw
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said there was no plan for the IAEA to inspect its damaged nuclear facilities, a day after Vice President JD Vance said conversations with inspectors could happen imminently.
